title: It Was Too Late For Man,
content:
It was too late for man,
    But early yet for God;
    Creation impotent to help,
    But prayer remained our side.

    How excellent the heaven,
    When earth cannot be had;
    How hospitable, then, the face
    Of our old neighbor, God!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "It Was Too Late For Man,", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
